Reuters Poll: S&P 500 Seen at 7,500 by End-2026

• 📊 Bullish Forecast: Reuters poll sees S&P 500 rising ~10% to ~7,500 by end-2026.

• 🏛️ Dow Outlook: Dow Jones projected at 52,000, signaling continued market strength.

• 💼 Growth Drivers: Solid earnings and steady economic expansion expected to support equities.

• ⚠️ Near-Term Risk: Strategists still expect a healthy correction amid volatility.

• 🤖 Tech Watch: AI disruption may keep tech volatile, but sector likely to lead earnings growth.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Near-term Volatile)
Long-term outlook remains bullish, though short-term market swings likely.

VIP Industries : Co Under GST Inspection by Maharashtra Authorities

• 🔍 Tax Action Initiated: Maharashtra State Tax department has started inspection/search proceedings at VIP Industries’ Nashik unit.

• 📅 Trigger Date: Action initiated on Feb 24, 2026 under GST Act provisions.

• 🤝 Company Cooperation: VIP stated it is extending full cooperation to authorities.

• 📊 No Impact Yet: Management says no material financial or operational impact currently.

🟠 Impact: Neutral to Negative
Regulatory scrutiny may weigh on sentiment in the near term, though no financial impact reported so far.

AI Spending Hype May Be Overdone: Wolfe Research

• ⚠️ Growth Doubts: Analysts warn AI capex expectations may be too optimistic for 2026.

• 📉 Market Sensitivity: AI headlines recently pushed major US indices down 1–1.6%, showing fragile sentiment.

• 🧱 Bottleneck Risks: Power, materials, and regulatory hurdles could slow AI buildouts by H2 2026.

• 🔄 Sector Rotation Risk: Any slowdown may hurt AI-heavy semis & industrials but support sectors previously pressured by AI fears.

🟠 Impact: Mixed to Negative
Near-term sentiment risk for AI-linked stocks; potential relief for non-AI sectors if spending cools.

Thyrocare ; Co Infuses Additional Capital into Tanzania JV

• 🌍 Overseas Expansion: Invests USD 4.75 million (~₹4.32 Cr) in Thyrocare Tanzania via CCPS.

• 🤝 JV Structure: Tanzania arm is a joint venture in diagnostic & healthcare services.

• 📊 Growth Funding: Capital aimed at business expansion and working capital needs.

• 🔁 Ongoing Commitment: Total investment in the subsidiary now ~USD 11.5 million.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Long-term)
Strengthens international presence and growth runway, though near-term financial impact may be limited.

Pharmaids Subsidiary Secures A2LA Accreditation

• 🧪 Key Approval: Material subsidiary Adita Bio Sys receives A2LA accreditation for medical device testing.

• 🌍 Global Compliance: Accreditation aligns with ISO/IEC 17025:2017 and US FDA GLP requirements.

• 📈 Capability Boost: Enhances credibility in biological testing and calibration services.

• 🚀 Business Potential: Strengthens positioning for regulated global markets and clients.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Accreditation improves regulatory standing and may support future business opportunities in testing services.

Anthropic launches enterprise agents program with ready-made plugins for businesses

Anthropic has unveiled its enterprise agents program.

 The new system lets companies deploy prebuilt Al agents for common tasks such as financial research, legal work, and HR operations. It builds on Claude Cowork. 

Companies can now set up private plugin marketplaces with controlled data flows and custom workflows. 

Stock plugins are available for finance, legal, and HR departments. Anthropic also added new connectors for Gmail, DocuSign, and Clay.

Biocon ; Co Gets USFDA Nod for Weight-Loss Drug Liraglutide

• 🇺🇸 Regulatory Win: Biocon receives USFDA approval for Liraglutide injection (Saxenda equivalent).

• 💉 Key Therapy: Drug targets chronic weight management under GLP-1 class.

• 📈 Growth Opportunity: Approval opens access to the large and fast-growing U.S. obesity market.

• 🚀 Commercial Push: Company plans early commercialization leveraging its integrated manufacturing platform.

🟢 Impact: Positive
US approval strengthens Biocon’s GLP-1 portfolio and supports growth in the high-value obesity segment.

reports.text:
🌟 What Do We Do With the Subscription and Donation Money? 🌟

You might be wondering — where does the subscription or donation money go? Here’s how we use it:

Every 2 months, we organize a donation drive where we:

🐶 Feed and care for stray dogs with food, medicine, and support
📚 Distribute stationery and essential items to students in need

All the subscription money, along with donations, is used for these drives. What may seem like a small contribution to us can mean the world to them. Your support can bring warmth, food, and comfort to a life that needs it.

Let’s join hands and make their days a little brighter — together! 💙🌸🐾

Service Care ; Co Receives Non-Binding LOI from Client

• 📩 LOI Received: Company gets non-binding Letter of Intent for a potential engagement.

• 🤝 Proposed Structure: Deal likely via Master Services Agreement (MSA) subject to further agreements.

• ⏳ Early Stage: Transaction subject to approvals and definitive agreement execution.

• 💰 No Immediate Impact: Company states no material financial impact currently.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Preliminary development; financial benefits depend on conversion of LOI into a binding contract.

Laurus Labs : Co Gets NCLT Nod for Composite Scheme

• ⚖️ NCLT Approval: NCLT Amaravati admitted Laurus’ application for the Composite Scheme of Arrangement.

• 🔄 Demerger Plan: Identified business of Laurus Synthesis Pvt Ltd (LSPL) to be demerged and merged with Sriam Labs Pvt Ltd.

• 🏢 Amalgamation Step: Remaining LSPL business to be amalgamated with Laurus Labs.

• 📅 Next Process: Meetings of unsecured creditors scheduled on April 6, 2026 for approval.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Procedural regulatory progress; financial impact depends on final implementation and synergy benefits.
Manilam Industries India Limited s IPO concluded, with a procurement amount of ₹ 168.61 crore and oversubscribed 5.89 times.

Capillary Tech ; Co to Acquire SessionM from Mastercard

• 🤝 Strategic Buy: Capillary Technologies to acquire SessionM Inc, a cloud-based loyalty & engagement platform.

• 💰 Deal Size: Base consideration of USD 20 million (subject to adjustments).

• 🌍 Global Expansion: Strengthens presence in North America & LATAM, complements APAC portfolio.

• 🧩 Business Synergy: Blue-chip client base and complementary model expected to unlock post-acquisition efficiencies.

• 📊 Target Profile: SessionM reported CY2025 turnover of USD 50.5 million.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Acquisition enhances Capillary’s global footprint and loyalty-tech capabilities, supporting long-term growth strategy.

Persistent Systems : Co Clarifies on M&A Speculation

• 🧾 Market Rumour Response: Company addressed speculation regarding a potential M&A opportunity.

• 🔍 Strategy Unchanged: Management reiterated it continues to explore inorganic growth as part of normal strategy.

• 🚫 No Immediate Deal: Company confirmed no imminent M&A transaction requiring disclosure at present.

• 📢 Transparency Assurance: Any future M&A requiring disclosure will be communicated as per regulations.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Clarification removes near-term speculation but indicates no immediate deal trigger; long-term inorganic strategy remains intact.

CAMS ; Co Partners PFRDA for NPS Vatsalya Awareness Drive

• 🤝 Strategic Tie-up: CAMS CRA partnered with PFRDA for NPS Vatsalya outreach in Tiruchirappalli.

• 🎯 Financial Literacy Push: Programme aims to promote early savings habits among families and students.

• 📊 Scheme Promotion: Initiative highlights NPS Vatsalya features for minors and long-term retirement planning.

• 🌐 Inclusion Focus: Reinforces CAMS’ role in nationwide financial inclusion and digital infra support.

• 🚀 Ecosystem Engagement: Outreach involved students, parents, teachers and officials to build awareness.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Sentimentally)
Strengthens CAMS’ positioning in the NPS ecosystem and supports long-term growth visibility through deeper financial inclusion initiatives.

AI Bubble Emerges as Top Concern for Credit Investors

• ⚠️ Rising Fear: 23% of IG investors now see AI overvaluation as the biggest risk (vs 9% earlier).

• 📉 Sentiment Shift: AI bubble worries overtake traditional credit and trade risks.

• 🧠 Obsolescence Risk Low: Only 10% fear AI-driven corporate disruption for now.

• 💰 Heavy Issuance Ahead: Hyperscaler bond issuance projected at $285B in 2026.

• 🔄 Cushioning Factor: Strong fund inflows expected to support bond markets.

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Slightly Negative
Rising bubble concerns signal valuation risks in AI-linked assets, but strong inflows may limit near-term credit market stress.

HealthCare Global (HCG) Approves ₹424.7 Cr Rights Issue

• 💰 Fund Raise Planned: Board approved rights issue up to ₹424.7 crore.

• 📊 Issue Size: 82.95 lakh shares to be issued via rights route.

• 🏷️ Issue Price: Rights shares priced at ₹512 per share (₹10 FV + ₹502 premium).

• 📅 Record Date: March 2, 2026 fixed to determine eligible shareholders.

• 🔢 Entitlement Ratio: 1 rights share for every 17 shares held.

• 📆 Timeline Set: Issue opens March 11 and closes March 25, 2026.

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Slightly Negative
While the fund raise strengthens the balance sheet, the rights issue may lead to near-term equity dilution and stock overhang.

Apollo Pipes ; Co Increases Stake in Subsidiary Kisan Mouldings

• 📊 Stake Hike: Apollo Pipes acquired additional 3.34% stake in Kisan Mouldings via secondary purchase.

• 🏢 Holding Rises: Company’s ownership increases from 58.60% to 61.94% in the subsidiary.

• 💰 Deal Size: Acquisition cost is approximately ₹9.8 crore (cash consideration).

• 🔧 Strategic Move: Investment aimed at strengthening presence in PVC pipes & fittings segment.

• 🚫 Not a Related Party Deal: Transaction executed at arm’s length.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Higher stake improves control over subsidiary and reinforces long-term strategic positioning in the pipes business.

Zelio E-Mobility ; Co Commissions New Odisha Plant; Capacity Jumps

• 🏭 New Plant Operational: Company sets up Cuttack (Odisha) facility to strengthen Eastern India presence.

• 📈 Capacity Expansion: Total installed capacity rises from 72,000 to 1,80,000 units/year; Odisha unit adds 60,000 units/year.

• 🚚 Efficiency Gains: Expected to reduce delivery time, cut logistics costs, and improve supply chain.

• 💰 Low Capex: Investment under ₹3 crore; plant already operational and revenue-accretive.

• 👥 Employment Boost: Facility to create 60–100 jobs, supporting regional expansion.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Capacity ramp-up with low investment improves growth visibility, strengthens regional reach, and supports scalability in the EV segment.

TATA STEEL : EU Sees Possible Relief in US Metals Tariffs

• 🇺🇸 Tariff Review Expected: EU anticipates the US may narrow the scope of 50% tariffs on steel & aluminum derivative products.

• 📦 Compliance Burden: Over 400 derivative items currently covered, creating complexity for exporters.

• ⚠️ Raw Metals Unchanged: Any easing likely won’t impact base steel & aluminum tariffs.

• 🤝 Ongoing Talks: EU and US in discussions to preserve last year’s trade deal amid policy uncertainty.

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Slight Positive
Potential easing could reduce trade friction and help exporters, but core metal tariffs remaining intact limits the overall benefit.

HG Infra ; Co Declared L1 Bidder for NHAI Road Project

• 🛣️ L1 Bid Win: HG Infra Engineering named lowest bidder by NHAI for a highway project in Odisha.

• 💰 Project Value: HG Infra bid cost at ₹1,582 Cr (NHAI estimate ₹1,827 Cr).

• 📍 Project Scope: Construction of 6-lane access-controlled capital region road (≈40.33 km) under HAM model.

• ⏱️ Execution Timeline: Project to be completed within 910 days.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Order win strengthens order book visibility and supports medium-term revenue growth.
EU EXPECTS US TO EASE IMPACT OF METALS TARIFFS IN COMING WEEKS

Lemon Tree Hotels ; Co Signs New Property in Uttar Pradesh

• 🏨 New Hotel Signing: Announced new Lemon Tree Hotel in Najibabad, UP, marking the company’s 25th property in the state.

• 📈 Portfolio Expansion: Currently operates 8 hotels in UP with 17 more in pipeline, reinforcing regional growth.

• 📍 Strategic Location: Najibabad offers access to Haridwar, Rishikesh & Jim Corbett, supporting tourism demand.

• 🛏️ Project Details: Upcoming hotel to feature 100 rooms with restaurant, banquet, conference and leisure amenities.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Strengthens presence in high-growth regional markets and supports long-term portfolio expansion.

Balkrishna Industries : Co Launches New Range of 2W & Truck Radial Tyres

• 🚀 Product Launch: Introduced new range of 2W tyres and Truck/Bus Radial tyres.

• 📅 Rollout Timeline:
– Media launch: 24 Feb 2026
– Channel partners: 25 Feb 2026
– Sales start: 1 Apr 2026

• 🚚 Segment Focus: Targets 2W, medium & heavy commercial truck tyre categories.

• 🇮🇳 Market Scope: Initially focused on domestic market.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Expands product portfolio and strengthens presence in key tyre segments, supporting future growth.

Panorama Studios ; Co Signs Line Producer Deal for Malayalam Film

• 🎬 New Project: Company signed a Line Producer Agreement with Locomotive Global Content LLP.

• 🎥 Film Details: Agreement is for producing Malayalam film “Chelembra Bank Heist” directed by Christo Tomy.

• 🌐 Regional Expansion: Strengthens presence in regional content production.

• 📈 Content Pipeline Boost: Adds to upcoming project slate, enhancing visibility.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Improves content pipeline and supports growth in regional film production business.

Panorama Studios : Co Signs Line Producer Deal for Malayalam Film

• 🎬 New Project: Company signed a Line Producer Agreement with Locomotive Global Content LLP.

• 🎥 Film Details: Agreement is for producing Malayalam film “Chelembra Bank Heist” directed by Christo Tomy.

• 🌐 Regional Expansion: Strengthens presence in regional content production.

🟢 Impact: Positive

United Polyfab ; Co Reports Fire Incident at Gujarat Plant

• 🔥 Fire Incident: Company reported a fire at Timba, Ahmedabad manufacturing facility on Feb 19, 2026.

• ⏱️ Temporary Disruption: Manufacturing operations were briefly impacted due to the incident.

• ✅ Operations Restored: Plant has been fully restored and is operational.

• 🛡️ Limited Impact: No long-term shutdown indicated by the company.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Short-term disruption but quick restoration limits financial and operational damage.
GIC RE : FSIB Recommends Hitesh Rameshchandra Joshi's Name For The Position Of CMD At GIC Re

SAMHI Hotels: Co Signs Deal with Marriott for Two New Hotels

• 🏨 Strategic Expansion: Subsidiary executed agreements with Marriott for two hotels in Navi Mumbai (Thane).

• 📍 Capacity Addition: Each project ~350 rooms, strengthening presence in key business corridor.

• ⭐ Premium Brand Tie-up: Properties to operate under Westin (upper upscale) and Fairfield by Marriott (upper mid-scale).

• 🤝 Partnership Deepens: Move further strengthens SAMHI–Marriott collaboration.

• 📈 Growth Visibility: Adds to pipeline and supports long-term room inventory expansion.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Brand-led expansion and inventory addition improve growth outlook and premium positioning.
BANK OF INDIA ; Supreme Court backs Bank of India in ₹1,500 crore loan default case, clarifies rules on bank ‘fraud’ classification

US Plans AI-Driven Pricing for Critical Minerals

• 🤖 AI Price Benchmarking: Pentagon/DARPA developing system to set fair global prices for key minerals.

• 🌍 Strategic Counter to China: Model will factor production costs and filter market distortions, targeting Chinese influence.

• 🧪 Initial Focus Minerals: Includes germanium, gallium, antimony, tungsten — critical for chips & defense.

• 🛡️ Tariffs + AI Strategy: Plan may combine AI pricing with tariffs to stabilize markets and support Western supply chains.

• ⚠️ Execution Uncertainty: Adoption, global acceptance, and trade retaliation risks remain unclear.

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Positive (Strategic)
Long-term supply chain positive for Western producers, but near-term global trade friction risk remains.
WHITE HOUSE ON IRAN: TRUMP'S FIRST OPTION IS ALWAYS DIPLOMACY BUT WILLING TO USE LETHAL FORCE WHEN AND IF NECESSARY

Pidilite Forms JV in Tanzania to Expand Overseas Presence

• 🌍 New JV Incorporated: Subsidiary Pidilite International and Insignia set up Pidilite Insignia Ltd in Tanzania.

• 🧪 Business Focus: JV will manufacture and market construction chemicals and waterproofing products.

• 📊 Majority Control: Pidilite arm to hold 55% stake, partner Insignia to hold 45%.

• 🚀 Africa Expansion: Move strengthens Pidilite’s international growth strategy, especially in emerging markets.

🟢 Impact: Positive
JV supports global expansion and long-term growth in high-potential African construction chemicals market.

WeWork India : Co Announces Capacity Expansion in Pune

• 🏢 Capacity Addition: Company signs Leave & License agreement to expand Pune space by ~1.63 lakh sq ft.

• 📈 Desk Expansion: Plans to add ~2,500 desks to meet rising demand.

• 🔥 High Utilization: Existing capacity utilization stands strong at ~83.9%.

• 💰 Investment Plan: Approx ₹36 crore capex, funded via internal accruals/debt.

• ⏳ Timeline: Additional capacity targeted on or before Aug 2026.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Expansion amid high utilization signals healthy demand and supports future revenue growth visibility.

Infosys : Co & Microsoft Complete Major Data Modernization for CSX

• 🤝 Strategic Collaboration: Infosys, with Microsoft, successfully delivered a large-scale data modernization program for CSX Corporation.

• 🧠 AI-Led Platform: Built using Infosys Topaz, Microsoft Fabric & Purview, enabling real-time analytics and governance.

• ⚙️ Operational Gains: Unified cloud-native platform replaced legacy systems, improving decision-making and reducing costs.

• 📊 Scalable Data Foundation: Created 170+ data products and enabled predictive analytics and logistics optimization.

• 🚀 AI Readiness Boost: Positions CSX for enterprise-wide intelligence and future AI-driven transformation.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Strengthens Infosys’ digital and AI credentials and reinforces partnership with Microsoft in large enterprise deals.

reports.text:
🗞 BEAT THE STREET BULLETIN 🗞
Date: 24 February 2026
Telegram: https://t.me/BeatTheStreetnews

📍 ORDER WIN

LARSEN & TOUBRO (L&T): Bags significant EPC order from Department of Atomic Energy for LIGO India Observatory project.

ADANI POWER: Subsidiary Moxie Power wins 558 MW PPA from Tamil Nadu Power Distribution Corp at ₹5.91/unit for 5 years.

BRAHMAPUTRA INFRASTRUCTURE: Emerges L1 bidder for ₹397 crore MoRTH highway widening project in Rajasthan (JV basis).

ORGANIC RECYCLING SYSTEMS: Wins EPC contract for 10 TPD CBG & biofertilizer plant in Madhya Pradesh.

📍 ACQUISITION

SURAJ ESTATE DEVELOPERS: Acquires 100% stake in Hally Pacific for ~₹30.4 crore; project GDV estimated at ₹200 crore in Mumbai.

BLUE CLOUD SOFTECH: To acquire 100% stake in Global Impex Inc via all-equity share swap deal.

KALPATARU PROJECTS INTERNATIONAL: To acquire remaining 35% stake in Saudi JV for SAR 10 million, making it wholly owned.

📍 COLLABORATION

SHILPA MEDICARE: Arm Shilpa Biologicals signs biosimilar licensing pact with SteinCares for Latin America markets.

CREATIVE NEWTECH: Partners with Kaspersky to expand cybersecurity distribution across India.

CWD LIMITED: Signs MoU with global enterprise software major to explore device-software integration alliance.

📍 LEADERSHIP CHANGES

SUZLON ENERGY: Appoints Ajay Kapur as Group CEO; forms Group Executive Council under Suzlon 2.0 strategy.

📍 NEW PROJECTS

CHALET HOTELS: Approves ₹632.8 crore investment for ~330-room luxury hotel project in Hyderabad.

SWAN DEFENCE & HEAVY INDUSTRIES: To complete 5 offshore support vessels at Pipavav shipyard for San Maritime.

📍 INCORPORATE / CORPORATE STRUCTURE

MOBIKWIK: Subsidiary Mobikwik Securities Broking receives BSE approval to commence stock broking operations from Feb 24, 2026.

📍 CLARIFICATION

C2C ADVANCED SYSTEMS: Issues clarification denying links to SEBI-related social media reports.

📍 ADDITIONS

ZELIO E-MOBILITY: Expands EV manufacturing capacity to 1.8 lakh units annually with Odisha plant commissioning.

FLAIR WRITING INDUSTRIES: Begins wooden pencil manufacturing in Surat with 84 million pieces annual capacity.

📍 Q2 / BUSINESS UPDATE

KAUSHALYA LOGISTICS: Handles 3.01 lakh MT cement volumes in Jan 2026, up 63% YoY.

📍 USFDA / REGULATORY

CAPLIN POINT LABORATORIES: Arm Caplin Steriles receives USFDA final approval for Sodium Phosphates Injection USP.

📍 OTHERS

IT STOCKS: Heavy put buying in Invesco Senior Loan ETF signals rising bearish bets on software-linked credit exposure.

TEXTILE SECTOR: DGFT cuts RoDTEP rates by 50%, raising margin pressure risks for exporters.

ADANI POWER: Bangladesh seeks to renegotiate power purchase deal over tariff concerns, reports suggest.

IT SECTOR: AI-driven automation seen disrupting COBOL modernisation and legacy IT services demand.

EUROPEAN IT SERVICES: BofA turns bullish, expects ~2.7% organic revenue growth in 2026.

---

📍 STAY UPDATED WITH BEAT THE STREET 🚀
For more updates, join our Telegram channel: https://t.me/BeatTheStreetnews

Suba Hotels Limited announces execution of TWO new hotel agreements under its asset light expansion strategy 💼🏨

📍 Gurugram, Haryana
✅ Revenue Share Agreement signed
✅ 58 room operational hotel
✅ Restaurant + Banquet + Meeting Room
✅ Swimming Pool + Training Hall
✅ Located in prime NCR business hub
✅ To operate under “Quality Inn” brand

📍 Click Hotel Vizag, Andhra Pradesh
✅ Hotel Management Agreement signed
✅ 32 room mid scale business hotel
✅ Banquet + Multi cuisine restaurant
✅ Strong presence in growing Vizag market
✅ Operated under “Click Hotel” brand

💡 Strategic Impact:
✔ Strengthening footprint in NCR & Andhra Pradesh
✔ Pure asset light growth model
✔ Long term revenue visibility
✔ Expanding branded portfolio across India
Northern ARC

reports.text:
Waaree Energies ; Co Bags LoA for 300MW Wind Project

• 🌬️ Order Win: Subsidiary Waaree Forever Energies receives Letter of Award from SECI.

• ⚡ Project Scope: Development of 300MW wind power project in Dwarka, Gujarat.

• 📅 Long-Term Visibility: Power Purchase Agreement valid for 25 years from supply commencement.

• 🇮🇳 Domestic Award: Contract awarded by Solar Energy Corporation of India (SECI).

• 🚀 Renewable Push: Strengthens Waaree’s presence in the wind energy segment.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Order enhances renewable project pipeline and improves long-term revenue visibility.

SAMHI Hotels : Co Gets KIADB Approval for Whitefield Expansion

• 🏨 Layout Approved: KIADB sanctioned layout plans for proposed building in Whitefield, Bengaluru.

• 📈 Capacity Expansion: Plan to add ~235 rooms alongside existing 142-room hotel.

• 🔢 Inventory Boost: Total complex capacity to rise to ~377 rooms in upper upscale/upscale segment.

• 🌆 Growth Market Focus: Strengthens presence in key Bengaluru hospitality market.

• 🏗️ Execution Pending: Development subject to project execution timelines.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Approval supports SAMHI’s capacity expansion and long-term revenue growth visibility in a high-demand micro-market.

SONA BLW ; BYD Pulls Ahead of Tesla in Europe EV Race

• 🚗 BYD Surge: January registrations jumped 165% YoY to 18,242 vehicles.

• 📊 Market Share Gains: BYD share rose to 1.9% in Europe.

• ⚠️ Tesla Weakness: Registrations fell 17% YoY to 8,075, share down to 0.8%.

• 🔄 Strategy Edge: BYD growth driven by both EV + hybrid portfolio.

• ⚡ EV Trend Strong: Electric vehicles now form 19.3% of EU car market despite overall auto slowdown.

🟢 Impact: 🔴 Negative for Tesla
BYD’s rapid scale-up signals rising competitive pressure on Tesla in Europe’s EV market.

POWERGRID ; Co Subsidiary Commissions Rajasthan Transmission Project

• ⚡ Project Commissioned: POWERGRID Narela Transmission Ltd successfully commissioned ISTS project.

• 🌞 Renewable Focus: Project strengthens evacuation of solar power from Rajasthan (8.1 GW zones).

• 🏗️ Won via TBCB: Project was secured through Tariff Based Competitive Bidding route.

• 📅 Commercial Start: Achieved commercial operation (DOCO) effective Dec 7, 2025.

• 📈 Execution Milestone: Enhances transmission network and supports India’s green energy push.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Project commissioning improves asset base and regulated revenue visibility, supporting long-term earnings stability.

Iykot Hitech Toolroom ; Aspect Global Ventures to Acquire 34.58% Stake in Lykot Hitech Toolroom

• 🤝 Promoter Stake Sale: Promoters to sell 35.89 lakh shares (34.58%) to Aspect Global Ventures.

• 💰 Deal Price: Transaction at ₹8 per share under Share Purchase Agreement.

• 🔔 Open Offer Trigger: Acquirer required to make mandatory open offer to public shareholders.

• 🏢 Change in Control: Post completion, acquirer to gain control and board representation.

• 📉 Capital Adjustment: Company had earlier forfeited and cancelled partly paid shares, recalculating voting capital.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Strategic investor entry and open offer expectation are sentimentally positive, though pricing at ₹8 may cap near-term upside.

TATA INVESTMENT: Decision On Extension Of N Chandrasekaran's Term Postponed Till Next Board Meeting In June - CNBC-TV18 

Tata Trusts Chairman Noel Tata Asked For Certain Conditions To Be Linked To N Chandrasekaran's Re-Appointment

Noel Tata Not In Favour Of Listing Tata Sons

With No Consensus On The Reappointment Both Noel Tata And N Chandra Sought Deferment Rather Than A Vote

N Chandrasekaran To Continue As Chairman Till February 2027

Hindalco ; Co Arm Novelis Enters Share Deal with AV Minerals

• 🤝 Strategic Transaction: Novelis (Hindalco subsidiary) signs subscription agreement with AV Minerals (Netherlands).

• 📊 Share Purchase: AV Minerals to buy 13,33,333 Novelis shares at $150 per share.

• 💰 Deal Size: Transaction valued at about $200 million (~₹1,660 crore).

• 🌍 Regulatory Filing: Novelis has filed Form 8-K with the US SEC.

• 🔄 Corporate Action: Transaction between shareholder entities; operational impact limited.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Primarily a shareholder-level transaction with no immediate impact on Hindalco/Novelis operations.

JP Morgan's Jamie Dimon Flags Pre-Crisis Style Risky Lending

• ⚠️ Risk Warning: Dimon says parts of the financial industry are repeating pre-2008 risky lending behavior.

• 📉 Credit Cycle Caution: While credit remains strong now, cracks are likely to emerge over time.

• 🤖 AI Disruption Risk: Flags AI as a potential disruptor, especially for software and tech sectors.

• 🏦 JPMorgan Strategy: Bank staying cautious on lending but competing aggressively in deals.

• 🎯 Long-Term Bet: JPMorgan positioning itself as a long-term AI winner despite rising volatility.

PHARMA ; Novo Nordisk to Slash Ozempic & Wegovy Prices in U.S.

• 💊 Major Price Cut: Company plans to reduce list prices by up to 50% for Ozempic and Wegovy.

• 📅 Effective Date: New pricing of $675/month to begin from Jan 1, 2027.

• 📉 Product-wise Impact: ~50% cut for Wegovy and ~34% reduction for Ozempic.

• 🔄 Wider Coverage: Price cuts will also apply to oral version Rybelsus.

• 🧭 Strategic Move: Aims to improve affordability and expand patient access, but may pressure margins.

Viviana Power Tech today looks very different from what it was a few years ago.

https://x.com/PULSEOFPROFIT10/status/2026201443687121292?s=20

Revenue scaled from ₹36 Cr (FY23) to ₹219 Cr (FY25).
FY26 revenue guidance: ₹500–560 Cr.
FY27 target: ₹850+ Cr.
Management guiding 40–50% CAGR over the next 2–3 years.

Current order book stands at ~₹1,400 Cr, with ~₹900 Cr under execution.
Company is bidding for ₹700–800 Cr additional projects and plans to add ₹500–600 Cr fresh T&D orders by Mar’26 / Q1 FY27.

Entry into BESS adds a new layer.
₹511 Cr secured (100 MW Rajasthan, 65 MW Gujarat).
~₹300 Cr capex (80% debt / 20% equity).
12-year annuity model with ~₹40 Cr potential annual revenue.
Expected returns in the 9–10% range.

H1 FY26 numbers show momentum:
Revenue +88% YoY
EBITDA +136% YoY
PAT +101% YoY
EBITDA margins ~19–20% standalone.
Consolidated margins stabilizing ~12%.
PAT margin guidance: 9–10% (protected via PV clauses).

ROE 33%+, ROCE ~35%.
Mainboard migration possible by Mar/Apr 2026.

Dr. Reddy : Co Gets SEC Relief in Anti-Corruption Probe

• ⚖️ Investigation Update: US SEC has concluded its investigation into alleged improper payments.

• ✅ No Enforcement Action: SEC does not intend to recommend any action against the company at present.

• 🌍 Regulatory Overhang Eases: Matter related to potential FCPA violations now sees reduced legal uncertainty.

• 📊 Compliance Cooperation: Company fully cooperated with SEC, DOJ and SEBI during the probe.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Removal of major regulatory overhang improves sentiment and reduces legal risk for Dr. Reddy’s.

IOL Chemicals : Co Gets EDQM CEP for Clopidogrel Besilate

• 🧪 Regulatory Approval: EDQM grants CEP certificate for API Clopidogrel Besilate on Feb 23, 2026.

• 🌍 Europe Market Access: Strengthens ability to supply to regulated European markets.

• 💊 Portfolio Expansion: Adds to existing CEP for Clopidogrel Bisulphate, enhancing product basket.

• 📈 Growth Potential: May support API exports and customer wins in cardiovascular segment.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Regulatory clearance improves market reach and strengthens pharma API growth prospects.

Tamboli Castings ; Co Initiates Miyawaki Forest Project in Bhavnagar

• 🌱 ESG Initiative: Tamboli Castings undertakes Miyawaki forest development over 13,000 sq. meters in Bhavnagar.

• 🌳 Large-Scale Plantation: Initial planting of 28,635 saplings using Miyawaki afforestation technique.

• 🤝 Collaborative Effort: Project executed under CSR with Rotary Club of Bhavnagar and Municipal Corporation.

• 🌍 Sustainability Focus: Reinforces company’s commitment to environmental stewardship and community well-being.

• ⏳ Long-Term Impact: Forest expected to evolve into a self-sustaining green ecosystem in 3–5 years.

🟢 Impact: Neutral to Positive
Strong ESG credentials but limited immediate financial impact on earnings.

Hexaware : Co Expands AWS Partnership to Launch AI-Powered SDLC Solutions

• 🤖 AI Engineering Push: Hexaware strengthens collaboration with AWS to deliver AI-enabled software development lifecycle (SDLC) solutions.

• ⚙️ RapidX Platform: Leverages AI-driven RapidX and Kiro IDE to convert AI-assisted coding into production-ready delivery.

• 🚀 Productivity Gains: Aims to reduce time-to-market, improve developer productivity, and modernize legacy apps.

• 🔐 Enterprise Focus: Solutions support secure deployment on AWS with private LLM options via Amazon Bedrock.

• 🌐 Cloud & AI Tailwind: Move aligns with rising enterprise demand for AI-led software engineering and cloud adoption.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Strategic AWS expansion strengthens Hexaware’s AI positioning and enhances growth visibility in digital engineering.

SCHAEFFLER INDIA Q3 RESULTS

Net Profit: ⬆️ 36% YoY at ₹ 322 Cr | ⬆️ 11% QoQ

Revenue: ⬆️ 28% YoY at ₹ 2,724 Cr | ⬆️ 12% QoQ

EBITDA: ⬆️ 31% YoY at ₹ 484.6 Cr | ⬆️ 6% QoQ

EBITDA Margin: 17.79% vs 17.37% (YoY) ⬆️ | 17.79% vs 18.73% (QoQ) ⬇️

@Beatthestreetnews

Essex Marine ; Co Launches Integrated Cold Chain Logistics Under ‘DE SENS COLDSTORE’

• 🚚 Strategic Shift: Company begins in-house cold chain logistics, moving from rental model to integrated platform.

• 📦 End-to-End Services: Will provide storage, transportation and distribution with full operational control.

• 🚛 Fleet Build-Up: Plans to lease 4 refrigerated vehicles and buy 12-ton refrigerated truck initially.

• 🌏 Regional Expansion: Operations to expand into North-East India (Siliguri → Guwahati).

• 📈 Revenue Potential: Expected to boost logistics revenues and improve supply-chain efficiency.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Forward integration strengthens value chain control, supports revenue growth, and improves operational efficiency.

Himadri Specialty : Co Starts 70,000 MTPA Carbon Black Line; Capacity Jumps

• 🏭 Commercial Operations Begin: Company starts 70,000 MTPA specialty carbon black line at Mahistikry plant.

• 📈 Capacity Expansion: Total carbon black capacity rises to 2,50,000 MTPA, with specialty capacity at 1,30,000 MTPA.

• 🌍 Global Scale: Mahistikry becomes world’s largest single-location specialty carbon black facility.

• 💼 Growth Visibility: Expansion to support premium applications like plastics, inks, paints and coatings.

• 🚀 Margin Tailwind: New capacity expected to boost revenue and improve margin profile over the medium term.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Large specialty capacity addition strengthens leadership, improves growth visibility, and supports margin expansion.

RVNL – ₹270.22 Cr Railway Order

• Wins Central Railway traction project
• Order value: ₹270.22 crore
• Execution timeline: 24 months
• Strengthens order book visibility

Meta Infotech – ₹2.46 Cr Order Win

• Secures cybersecurity renewal order
• Order value: ₹2.46 crore
• Tenure till Feb 2027
• Supports revenue visibility

RBZ Jewellers ; Co Receives ₹1.01 Cr Insurance Claim for Earlier Fraud

• 💰 Claim Received: Company received ₹1.01 crore from IFFCO Tokio towards partial settlement of fraud loss.

• 📊 Total Recovery: Aggregate recovery now stands at ₹1.24 crore.

• ⚠️ Earlier Impact: Fraud impact earlier estimated at ₹1.98 crore; net hit reduces after claim.

• 🔍 Further Action: Company continues efforts to recover the remaining amount and is cooperating with authorities.

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Slightly Positive
Insurance recovery reduces financial damage, but the underlying fraud incident remains an overhang.

Intellect Wins ; Co Strategic AI Deal from Leading Financial Group

• 🤝 Major Mandate: Leading Indian financial services group selects Purple Fabric platform for enterprise AI transformation.

• 🏦 Multi-Entity Rollout: Initial deployment across five entities, enabling enterprise-wide AI adoption.

• 🚀 Strengthens Positioning: Reinforces Intellect’s momentum in governed enterprise AI for BFSI segment.

• 🧠 Platform Capabilities: Expected to enhance operational intelligence, governance, risk & compliance workflows.

• 🔍 Competitive Win: Deal secured after an 8-month competitive evaluation process.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Large enterprise AI win improves deal visibility, strengthens BFSI franchise, and supports long-term growth momentum.

Aurobindo Pharma : Co Incorporates New Wholly Owned Subsidiary

• 🏢 New Entity Formed: Company incorporates Egenra Biologics Pvt Ltd as a wholly owned subsidiary.

• 🧬 Strategic Purpose: Move aimed at expanding contract manufacturing and pharma operations.

• 💰 Investment Size: Initial capital subscription of ₹10 lakh (100% stake held).

• 📍 Fresh Incorporation: Newly formed entity; no historical turnover available.

• ✅ Regulatory Note: No additional government approvals required.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Mild)
Supports long-term manufacturing expansion strategy, though near-term financial impact remains limited.

Game Changers Texfab ; Co to Incorporate New Subsidiary

• 🏢 New Subsidiary: Board approves incorporation of Game Changers Attirex Pvt Ltd.

• 👕 Business Focus: Subsidiary to undertake manufacturing, sourcing & supply of uniforms and textile products.

• 📊 Strategic Fit: Move aimed at diversifying textile/apparel verticals and strengthening institutional uniform segment presence.

• 💰 Capital Structure: Proposed paid-up capital of ₹10 lakh; company to hold ~80% stake.

• 🗓️ Timeline: Incorporation expected within 15–20 days, subject to regulatory filings.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Mild)
Strategic diversification into apparel/uniform segment supports long-term growth, though financial impact is small initially.

Vraj Iron & Steel : Co Approves Bilaspur Rolling Mill Expansion

• 🏭 Capacity Expansion: Board approves new rolling mill at Bilaspur to manufacture TMT bars.

• 📦 New Capacity: Planned installed capacity of 1.5 lakh tons per annum.

• 💰 Project Cost: Estimated investment of ₹35 crore (plus GST); cost may vary by up to 15%.

• 🗓️ Timeline: Capacity addition targeted in FY27.

• 💳 Funding Plan: To be funded via internal accruals and possible promoter loan up to ₹10 crore.

• 📈 Strategic Rationale: Aims to boost finished steel output, improve efficiency, and strengthen TMT market position.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Capacity expansion supports future growth, scale benefits, and revenue visibility in the TMT segment.

Meta Infotech Bags ₹2.46 Cr Cybersecurity Order

• 🧾 Order Win: Company secures renewal order from a domestic financial services client.

• 🔐 Scope: Subscription of software licenses for endpoint protection and cloud workload security.

• 💰 Order Size: Contract valued at ~₹2.46 crore (excluding GST).

• 📅 Tenure: Contract valid from Feb 20, 2026 to Feb 19, 2027.

• 🔄 Business As Usual: Order received in the normal course of business.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Strengthens order pipeline and supports steady revenue visibility in the cybersecurity segment.

Vraj Iron & Steel – Capacity Expansion

• New rolling mill at Bilaspur
• Capacity addition: 1.5 lakh TPA
• Project cost: ₹35 crore (+/-15%)
• Target completion: FY27
• Funded via internal accruals & promoter loan
• Positive for long-term scale

Blue Jet Healthcare Ltd – Management Interview Highlights (NDTV Profit)

Strategic Vision – Decade of Blitzscale Growth

• Management sees strong structural tailwinds in Pharma & API sectors
• Targeting aggressive growth over the next 10 years
• New R&D center under construction in Hyderabad
• R&D capacity to double post completion

Vizag Facility – ₹1,000 Crore Phase 1 Capex

• Total planned investment: ₹1,000 crore
• Groundbreaking on 28 February (Q4 FY26)
• Operational readiness in 2–2.5 years (Q4 FY28–Q1 FY29)

• Land parcel: 102 acres
• Structured into 4 production blocks
• Andhra Pradesh offers single-window regulatory clearance

Capacity Allocation Strategy – Focus on High Asset Turns (~4x)

• Targeting ~4x asset turns through pre-secured client commitments
• Contrast Media – 2 production blocks

• Rapid ramp-up to ~80% utilization

• High-Intensity Sweeteners – 1 production block
• Anchor client secured
• Initial utilization projected at 40–50% within 1–2 years

Commercial Execution Plan

• Existing volumes to be migrated to Vizag facility post commissioning
• Pursuing 2 high-conviction lateral entry opportunities
• Focus on commercial, non-clinical products
• Objective: Strong production ramp-up from day one

HEG Ltd – Strategic Partnership for EV Materials

• Subsidiary TACC partners with INOX Air Products
• Dedicated Nitrogen plant at Dewas facility
• Focus on Lithium-ion battery grade graphite anode material
• Strengthens EV and energy storage play
• Long-term operational positive

Government Halves RoDTEP Benefits, Exporters Brace for Impact

The government has cut benefits under the Remission of Duties and Taxes on Exported Products (RoDTEP) scheme by 50% with immediate effect.

Applicable RoDTEP rates will now be capped at half of the existing rates, including notified value caps.

Earlier, refunds ranged from 0.3% to 4.3% of the value of exported products; these will now fall to 0.15% to 2.15%.

RoDTEP reimburses taxes, duties and levies incurred at central, state and local levels that are not refunded under other mechanisms.

Refunds are issued as transferable duty credit scrips, which exporters can use to pay import duties or sell in the market.

The move has raised concerns among exporters already navigating a challenging global trade environment.

France May Lose Tax Edge as India Scraps MFN Clause in DTAA

India plans to remove the Most Favoured Nation (MFN) clause from its Double Taxation Avoidance Convention (DTAC) with France, ending capital gains tax benefits for France-based FPIs.

If implemented, French portfolio investors in Indian markets will have to pay capital gains tax in India.

France-based FPIs held about $21 billion in Indian equities as of January 2026.

The move could generate significant additional tax revenue for India.

Several FPIs had routed investments via France after India tightened tax treaties with Mauritius and Singapore in 2017.

Some investments are also routed through Participatory Notes (P-notes) issued by Sebi-registered FPIs.

Under the current treaty, FPIs from France with up to a 10% stake in Indian firms pay no capital gains tax due to earlier bilateral provisions.

The proposed change signals India’s broader effort to plug tax arbitrage routes and align foreign investment taxation across jurisdictions.

Tata Motors, M&M Indonesia Deal Stalls Amid Import Backlash

A proposed ₹25 trillion Indonesian Rupiah (~$1.5 billion) truck procurement deal involving Tata Motors and Mahindra & Mahindra has faltered due to domestic opposition in Indonesia.

The contract, one of the largest global supply deals, involved orders for 35,000 four-wheel-drive pickups and 35,000 six-wheel trucks from each company.

State-owned Agrinas Pangan Nusantara had placed orders as part of Indonesia’s food security and rural logistics programme.

Indonesia’s automotive lobby strongly opposed large-scale imports of completely built units (CBUs), arguing it would hurt local manufacturers already facing slowing demand.

A local think tank (Celios) warned the move could shrink Indonesia’s GDP by $2.3 billion and displace up to 330,000 workers.

Indonesia’s automotive sales are expected to decline about 6.8% in 2025, intensifying concerns over competition from imports, especially cheaper unofficial Chinese imports.

Industry bodies argue local firms can meet demand if given time, pushing the government to reconsider the import-heavy proposal.

CITI on Bharti Airtel

Buy, TP Rs 2380

Bharti’s NBFC expansion, anchored by a planned Rs200bn capital infusion over the next few years (70% contribution by Bharti Airtel, 30% by promoter entity Bharti Enterprises), is a natural adjacency to develop its next growth engine and diversify its portfolio.

Over past two years, Bharti has built a strong lending service provider model by integrating technology, data, and customer insights.

It now intends to integrate this capability with its NBFC and scale the business using its balance sheet.

Given Bharti’s strong FCF generation and upcoming rights issue payment, this foray should not materially weigh on leverage or cash flows

That said, achieving optimal capital utilisation with efficient risk‑adjusted returns and meaningful scale typically takes a few years for lending platforms.

Kotak Inst Eqt on ITC

Reduce, TP Rs 338

ITC’s blended 25% price hike on cigarettes—well below the ~35% required to offset tax increase—could result in a 30-33% near-term decline in cigarettes EBIT

This reflects a deliberate trade-off: prioritizing volume protection over profitability through staggered price increases.

More importantly, ITC—market and price leader—waited for Godfrey Philips (GPI) to announce Marlboro Compact price hike before matching it

This underscores heightened intensity of competition in RSFT segment & highlights ITC’s growing sensitivity to market share loss to Marlboro

CLSA on ITC

O-P, TP cut to Rs 367 from Rs 485

Indirect tax on cigarettes increased considerably in February 2026, with replacement of compensation cess by GST and excise duties

Believe ITC will need price increases of 33% to be neutral on Ebit per cigarette, which would drag volumes and cigarette division Ebit in FY27

Expect a recovery in FY28 after assuming no further tax increases, as suggested by ITC’s historical record in passing on these tax increases (last in FY14-16)

Cut estimates by 4-28%

IBM stock dipped more than 10% once Anthropic went public with Claude's new ability to optimize legacy COBOL code.

"SaaSocalypse." is in full swing, the death of the "Per-Seat" model of Software.

IBM has a massive business built on "modernizing" old systems. It’s slow, expensive, human-heavy work. 

When Anthropic shows that Claude can map out and fix that old code in a fraction of the time, serious question arising on IBM’s "army of humans" model to create per-seat revenue.

Investors are terrified, the market is realizing that if the AI is the "worker," the old software subscription models are basically broken.
https://claude.com/blog/how-ai-helps-break-cost-barrier-cobol-modernization

Anthropic AI tweets 

We’ve identified industrial-scale distillation attacks on our models by DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax.

These labs created over 24,000 fraudulent accounts and generated over 16 million exchanges with Claude, extracting its capabilities to train and improve their own models.

If those weaken while AI capability keeps accelerating, the displacement scenario becomes real.

The risk is not AI failing.
The risk is AI working faster than institutions can adjust.

🚨 AI IS ABOUT TO TRIGGER THE BIGGEST ECONOMIC CRISIS SINCE 2008.

Not because AI is failing. But because AI is moving at a pace the traditional economy is not adjusting to fast enough.

This is what Citrini layed out in its research and here's everything you need to know about it.

White collar workers make up roughly half of US employment. But more importantly, the top 20% of income earners drive around 60%+ of total consumer spending in the US.

That means a relatively small group supports a very large part of:
• housing demand
• car purchases
• travel
• restaurants
• software subscriptions
• private school
• mortgage quality

Now AI coding tools are already reducing the cost of building software. Companies are renegotiating large SaaS contracts because internal teams using AI can now replicate core features faster and cheaper.

When companies cut 10-15% of staff, seat based software revenue drops automatically.

EARLY IMPACT: Margins improve because payroll drops.

SECOND IMPACT: High-income wages weaken.

THIRD IMPACT: Spending slows.

But machines produce output. Machines do not consume output. If a $180,000 product manager role is replaced by a $200/month AI tool, corporate profit rises but household income falls.

If this happens at scale, you get a loop:

AI improves → companies cut jobs → spending weakens → companies protect margins → buy more AI → cut more jobs.

Each step makes sense for one company. But across the whole economy, it shrinks the wage base that supports demand.

Now look at what just happened. After Claude announced its new Code Security AI update, cybersecurity stocks sold off sharply.

Why? Because investors immediately realized AI is now automating tasks inside cybersecurity itself, a sector that was considered protected from automation risk.

That is the displacement effect spreading beyond software into security, compliance, and enterprise defense roles. This is how the loop expands.

Now add credit.

US private credit has grown to over $2 trillion. Many deals in software and tech were priced assuming stable recurring revenue. If AI compresses pricing power, those revenue assumptions weaken.

If defaults rise, private credit funds, insurers, and pension holders face pressure.

Now look at housing. The US mortgage market is around $13 trillion. Mortgages are underwritten on stable income assumptions.

If high income job stability weakens, lenders tighten standards. If housing demand weakens while incomes are under pressure, prices soften.

That affects household wealth and confidence.

Now look at government revenue. The US government collects most of its money from payroll taxes and income taxes. If labor income shrinks while capital income rises, tax collection changes.

At the same time, displaced workers need support. That means higher spending and lower tax receipts at the same time.

This is different from 2008.
2008 was a financial system failure.
This would be a labor structure shift.
In past tech revolutions, new jobs replaced old jobs quickly.

The risk here is speed.

If AI replaces high-income cognitive roles faster than new high-income roles are created, spending drops before the system adapts.

This does not guarantee collapse. But it increases the risk of:

• weaker discretionary spending
• pricing pressure in software and services
• rising stress in private credit
• housing sensitivity in high-income cities
• fiscal deficits widening during a productivity boom

AI can be extremely productive and still create short-term economic instability. The system was built on scarce human intelligence.

If intelligence becomes cheap and widely available through machines, the value of human labor changes. Markets will reprice that shift.

If you want to track whether this risk is building, watch simple data:

• white collar job openings
• wage growth in high-income sectors
• enterprise software renewal pricing
• private credit default rates
• housing delinquencies in tech-heavy metros
• payroll tax receipts

The Global AI Crisis - Citrini Research

Microfinance sector is showing signs of recovery

• 🔄 Order Nature: One-time supply contract in the normal course of business.

• 🔒 Governance Comfort: No promoter or related-party involvement.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Large module order strengthens order visibility and reinforces Waaree’s position in the solar supply chain.

reports.text:
India’s data center power demand is set to 6x by 2030, hitting 45 TWh! 
Capacity is surging to 10GW, potentially straining grids. AI growth needs urgent infra upgrades. 🇮🇳💻

Dhruv Consultancy ; Co Wins NHAI Supervision Contract

• 🛣️ LOA Received: Company secures Supervision Consultancy (SC) order from NHAI (Hyderabad).

• 📍 Project Scope: O&M supervision for 85.8 km stretch of NH-765 in Telangana (PMC mode).

• 💰 Order Value: Contract worth ₹2.88 Cr (ex-GST).

• ⏳ Execution Timeline: Project to be completed over 36 months.

• 🔒 No Related Party: Order awarded in normal course; no promoter interest involved.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Order win strengthens order book visibility, though ticket size is relatively small.
EU Commission Spokesperson: Commissioner Sečević is to meet with G7 trade ministers on Monday afternoon to discuss tariffs

Aequs : Co to Acquire 50% Stake in Aequs Foundation

• 🤝 Strategic Stake Buy: Board approves acquisition of 1,000 equity shares (50%) in Aequs Foundation via secondary deal.

• 🧾 Related Party Deal: Transaction classified as related-party but executed on arm’s length basis.

• 🎯 Purpose: Move aimed at improving visibility and control over CSR initiatives of the group.

• 💰 Deal Size: Total consideration of ₹10,000 (cash) — financially immaterial.

• ⏳ Timeline: Acquisition expected to be completed within one month of approval.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Transaction is small and CSR-focused; limited direct financial impact on Aequs’ core business.

Tiger Logistics Expands Western India Presence Amid Solar EXIM Boom

• 📍 Strategic Expansion: Strengthens Gujarat presence, a key solar EXIM hub.

• 🚚 Volume Growth Visibility: Tigreen vertical handles ~2,000 TEUs/month, with Gujarat contributing the majority.

• 📈 Strong Outlook: Solar container volumes expected to grow 40–45% YoY.

• 👔 Leadership Strengthening: Appoints General Manager – Gujarat to boost execution and customer reach.

• ☀️ Sector Tailwinds: Expansion aligned with India’s rising solar manufacturing and export momentum.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Positions Tiger Logistics to capture rising solar EXIM demand and supports medium-term volume growth visibility.

COLGATE ; Colgate Palmolive Partners Haryana Govt for Oral Health Program

• 🤝 Strategic Tie-up: Partners with Haryana Government under Bright Smiles, Bright Futures initiative.

• 🎯 Large Outreach: Program to cover 57 lakh school children across the state.

• 📚 Preventive Focus: Aims to embed oral health education in public & private school systems.

• 🌍 Strong Brand Visibility: Expands Colgate’s flagship social initiative footprint in India.

• 📈 Long-Term Engagement: Supports sustained consumer connect and brand recall among young users.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Sentimentally)
Strengthens brand equity and long-term market presence, though immediate financial impact is limited.

BHARTI AIRTEL: Bharti Airtel Plans Big NBFC Expansion via Airtel Money

• 💰 Large Capital Infusion: Airtel to invest ₹20,000 Cr to scale its NBFC platform.

• 🏦 RBI License Secured: Airtel Money received NBFC registration on Feb 13, 2026.

• 📊 Strong Early Traction: Lending platform already crossed ₹9,000 Cr disbursements with healthy asset quality.

• 📱 Digital Advantage: To leverage Airtel’s large customer base, data analytics and LSP model.

• 🚀 New Growth Engine: Move aimed at deepening financial inclusion and diversifying revenue streams.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Aggressive NBFC scale-up can unlock a new high-growth fintech vertical for Airtel and improve long-term earnings visibility.

Talbros Auto Gets MCA Nod for JV with Lohum CleanTech

• 🤝 JV Progress: MCA approves incorporation of JV Lohum Talbros CarbonTech Pvt Ltd.

• 🔋 Strategic Move: JV formed with Lohum CleanTech to enter advanced carbon/cleantech space.

• 📜 Regulatory Milestone: Approval received on Feb 22, 2026, advancing earlier board decision.

• 🚀 Future Opportunity: Expected to strengthen presence in EV/clean mobility value chain.

🟢 Impact: Positive
JV approval opens new growth avenue in the emerging clean-tech and battery ecosystem.

TCC Concept Gets NSE Listing & Trading Approval

• 📜 NSE Approval: Company receives listing and trading approval from NSE.

• 🧾 Shares Listed: 4.75 crore equity shares approved for trading.

• 🏷️ Trading Symbol: Will trade on NSE under the symbol “TCC”.

• 📅 Effective Date: Listing to be effective from February 25, 2026.

• 📈 Liquidity Boost: Dual listing expected to improve stock visibility and investor participation.

🟢 Impact: Positive
NSE listing enhances liquidity, market reach, and institutional visibility for the stock.

Fractal : Co Launches ‘PiEvolve’ Agentic AI Engine; Achieves Top ML-Bench Ranking

• 🤖 New AI Launch: Fractal unveils PiEvolve, an agentic engine for autonomous ML and scientific discovery.

• 🏆 Benchmark Milestone: Ranked among top-performing agents on OpenAI’s ML-Bench, crossing key performance thresholds.

• ⚡ Advanced Capability: Designed for continuous self-improvement, reasoning, code generation, and validation loops.

• 🌐 Enterprise Focus: Targets complex use cases across supply chains, financial services, and data-driven operations.

• 📈 Innovation Push: Strengthens Fractal’s positioning in the fast-growing enterprise AI and agentic AI space.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strategic)
Enhances technology leadership and long-term growth narrative, though near-term financial impact may be limited.

Ashish Dhawan holds stocks worth of Rs. 1,987

Top 5 stocks account for 80% of Portfolio

Yash Innoventures : Co Announces ₹120 Cr Real Estate Project Update

• 🏗️ Project Expansion: Company undertaking real estate development project of ~₹120 crore.

• 🏠 Land Aggregation Progress: Completed acquisition of 24 residential flats as part of land assembly.

• 📜 Key Approval Received: Obtained Non-Agricultural (NA) permission for the project land.

• 📊 Growth Milestone: Move marks company’s expansion into real estate development segment.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Early Stage)
Strategically positive for growth visibility, but execution and monetisation remain key monitorables.

Prostarm ; Co Bags ₹13.43 Cr Solar Project Order from South Eastern Railway

• 🏆 Order Win: Prostarm receives Letter of Acceptance (LOA) from South Eastern Railway.

• ☀️ Project Scope: Supply, installation, and erection of Solar Power Plant at SRC jurisdiction (Phase 2).

• 💰 Order Value: Contract size stands at ₹13.43 crore.

• ⏳ Execution Timeline: Project to be executed within 12 months from LOA date.

• 🔧 Long-Term Support: Includes 5-year CMC, adding service revenue visibility.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Marginal)
Strengthens order book and renewable portfolio, though order size is relatively small.

Patel Engineering JV Wins ₹133 Cr Irrigation Project as L1 Bidder

• 🏆 L1 Win: Patel Engineering (with JV partner) declared lowest bidder for ₹133.25 Cr irrigation project from MKVDC.

• 💰 Company Share: Patel Engineering’s share in the contract stands at ₹67.96 Cr.

• 🚧 Project Scope: Includes construction of head works, pump houses, pipelines, and allied civil & electro-mechanical works.

• ⏳ Execution Timeline: Project to be completed within 48 months.

• 🌾 Irrigation Boost: Project to serve ~2,277 hectares irrigation command area in Satara district, Maharashtra.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Order win strengthens order book visibility, though size is relatively moderate.

HFCL : HFCL Joins IIT Delhi–Led Consortium for Hollow-Core Fiber Tech

• 🤝 Strategic Collaboration: HFCL joins DoT-funded consortium led by IIT Delhi to develop next-gen hollow-core fiber (HCF).

• ⚡ Advanced Technology: HCF expected to reduce latency and energy consumption vs conventional optical fiber.

• 🌐 Future Networks Focus: Technology seen as critical for 6G, AI data centers, quantum networks and ultra-low-latency infra.

• 🏭 Capability Boost: HFCL to contribute manufacturing and application expertise for commercial deployment pathway.

• 🇮🇳 Indigenous Push: Initiative aims to strengthen India’s self-reliance in advanced optical communication tech.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strategic)
Enhances HFCL’s positioning in next-gen telecom technologies, though financial gains likely medium-to-long term.

Transindia Real Estate ; Co Signs MoU for Rail-Connected ICD Project

• 🤝 Strategic MoU: Transindia Real Estate (TREL) signs MoU with Allcargo Inland Park (AIPPL) and Allcargo Terminals (ATL).

• 🚆 Logistics Infra Plan: Proposal to develop and operate a Private Freight Terminal / Rail-Connected ICD at Gurugram, Haryana.

• 🏗️ Asset Monetisation: AIPPL’s land to be leveraged for logistics infrastructure development.

• 💰 Recurring Income Potential: Arrangement expected to generate stable lease income with periodic escalation.

• 📊 Early-Stage Deal: MoU is non-binding; detailed agreements subject to approvals.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Long Term)
Strengthens logistics ecosystem and land monetisation visibility, though near-term impact limited until final execution.

Market Closes In Green; IT Weak, Midcaps Lag

• 📈 Benchmark Gains: Sensex up 480 pts to 83,295, Nifty up 142 pts to 25,713.

• 🟢 Overall Positive: Broader market closed largely in the green with Nifty above 25,700.

• 📉 Midcap Pressure: Midcap index fell 258 pts to 59,256, showing underperformance.

• 🏦 Bank Support: Nifty Bank rose 92 pts to 61,264, lending support to markets.

• 💻 IT Weakness: Nifty IT declined for the third straight session.

• 🔻 Top Losers: IDFC First Bank, UPL, AU SFB among key laggards.

Anondita Medicare Ltd. is showing strong operating momentum with improving margins and scale benefits.

H1 FY26 revenue grew 72% YoY to ₹5,410L, while PAT rose 116% to ₹1,302L. EBITDA margin expanded to 35.5% and PAT margin stood at 24%, reflecting solid operating leverage.

FY25 revenue was ₹7,699L with PAT of ₹1,642L, indicating a sharp improvement over previous years.

Capacity expansion is underway with existing 562 mn units and additional 1,360 mn units approved under ₹75 Cr capex. Trial production has already begun.

Global certifications like MDSAP and SABS strengthen export potential, with UNFPA qualification in advanced stages.

reports.text:
Marathon Nextgen Q3 & 9M FY26 Earnings Highlights

Financial Performance  
- Highest ever PAT ₹161 Cr.  
- Driven by strong commercial portfolio + steady residential.  

9M FY26 Metrics  
- Total revenue ₹487 Cr.  
- Area sales: 1.8 lakh sq ft (pre-merger), 2.46 lakh sq ft (post-merger).  
- Booking value: ₹421 Cr (pre-merger), ₹628 Cr (post-merger).  
- Collections: ₹578 Cr (pre-merger), ₹798 Cr (post-merger).  
- Net debt-free balance sheet.  

Key Takeaway  
Record ₹161 Cr PAT + debt-free status underscore Marathon Nextgen's robust execution.
Share India Securities Limited 🚀 has approved an additional investment of ₹5.99 Crore (₹5,99,39,940) in its subsidiary, Share India Cred Capital Private Limited (SICCPL).

eMudhra Surge: PQC Readiness Demand

Quantum Risk Rise  
- Surge in global enterprise demand for Post-Quantum Cryptography advice.  
- Transition strategies needed as quantum computing advances.  

Threat Escalation  
- Legacy crypto algorithms at risk.  

Regulatory Push  
- Standards bodies urging quantum threat assessments.  
- Focus on long-lived data & digital identities.  

Boardroom Priority  
- Executives seek clarity on PQC timelines, risks, migration paths.  

Key Takeaway  
Quantum threat drives eMudhra's PQC consulting boom.

IDFC First Bank Chandigarh-Haryana Fraud Concall

Fraud Nature  
- Limited to one Chandigarh branch, Haryana govt-linked accounts.  
- Modus: Forged cheques/debit instructions processed via employee-external collusion.  
- Classic physical cheque fraud; bypassed maker-checker/AML controls.  
- No senior mgmt involvement; implicated staff suspended.  

Quantum & Mitigants  
- Headline impact ₹590 Cr (₹490 Cr discrepancy + ₹100 Cr suspect accounts).  
- Conservative estimate; minor adjustments expected.  
- Recovery: Lien/blocks on traced funds, ₹35 Cr insurance.  
- Legal sharing pending forensic/legal process.  
- Q4 P&L hit via provisions; still profitable, above ₹500 Cr PAT run-rate.  

Deposit Risks  
- Haryana deposits 0.5% total; ₹200 Cr outflow manageable.  
- Govt/PSU deposits 8-10% (industry norm).  
- Deep govt integrations (GST/CBDT) to limit contagion.  
- Some de-panelments expected; focus on responsibility acceptance.  

Controls Failure & Fixes  
- Existing: Maker-checker, ₹5L/10L verifications, AML/mule alerts.  
- Failed due to collusion on physical cheques.  
- Enhancements: Mandatory app alerts for high-value, AI signature verification, staff monitoring, govt balance reconciliation.  

Forensic & Timeline  
- KPMG appointed; 4-5 weeks for report.  
- Police complaints filed, liens initiated, board/RBI informed.  

Mgmt Tone & Guidance  
- First major incident in 10 years; "eye-opener".  
- Core metrics strong: Op profit margin >2%, NIM 5.7-5.8%, falling credit costs.  
- Well-capitalised (₹46,000 Cr NW); Q4 growth intact.  
- Own faults, pursue others; "look through" to profitability trajectory.  

Key Takeaway  
₹590 Cr ring-fenced fraud provisionable in Q4 without derailing franchise or growth.

India Export Relief: US Tariff Drops to 15%

Tariff Backstory  
- India faced 50% US tariff (25% reciprocal + 25% for Russian oil).  
- India-US interim trade deal this month cut it to 18%.  
- Supreme Court invalidated Trump's tariff orders entirely.  
- Trump invoked Section 122 Trade Act for flat 15% global tariff on all imports, effective Feb 26.  

Impact for Indian Exporters  
- Down from 50% to 15% - massive improvement.  
- ~55% of India's US-bound exports freed from 18% reciprocal duty.  
- Revert to standard MFN rates.  

Benefiting Sectors  
- Textiles.  
- Marine products (shrimp).  
- Pharma.  
- Gems.  

Key Takeaway  
15% flat tariff + MFN reversion breathes life into textiles, gems, pharma exporters.

RBI Norms to Hit Banks' Fee Income

Key Impacts  
- Draft guidelines target mis-selling of financial products.  
- Tighter broker funding norms expected to dent non-interest income.  

Bancassurance Exposure FY25  
- HDFC Bank: ₹6,308 Cr (13.8% other income).  
- Axis Bank: ₹3,174 Cr (12.5%).

Mis-Selling Rules  
- Prohibit bundling third-party products with core offerings.  
- Require explicit customer consent.  
- Ensure product suitability, transparent disclosures.  
- Mandate compensation for mis-selling.  

Broker Funding Tightening  
- No funding brokers for proprietary trades.  
- Back exposures with 100% collateral (significant cash component).  
- Broker guarantees need 50% collateral, min 25% cash.  

Timeline & Effects  
- Effective from July; final framework in February.  
- May reduce float income, commissions; hits large private banks harder.  

Key Takeaway  
RBI curbs on mis-selling & broker funding to squeeze bancassurance/commission streams for banks.

**Exposure, deposits and quantification
**• Haryana-related deposits constitute ~0.5% of total bank deposits.
• The observed outflow from these accounts is approximately INR 2bn.
• The total exposure is estimated at ~INR 5.9bn, comprising:
o INR 4.9bn identified initially, and
o An additional ~INR 1bn identified subsequently.
• Management has clearly communicated the outer limit of the incident without delay.
• Government deposits (state + central) typically account for 8–10% of overall deposits.
 
**Detection, background and timeline**
• The bank was first notified by the Haryana State Government, with the issue gaining momentum post 18 February.
• One customer highlighted discrepancies in account statements, after which the bank reconciled other related accounts.
• There was no error in system-generated statements; alerts, SMS notifications and transaction statements were duly sent.
• No communication has been received from any other state government regarding similar issues.
 
**Nature of the incident
**• The incident is limited to a single branch and involved multiple accounts.
• This was not a system failure but a case of employee collusion with third parties.
• The transactions were executed through the cheque-based system, not electronic channels.
• The bank has stated this is a one-off incident, given its otherwise strong operating track record.
• The incident has deeply disturbed management, and decisive steps will be taken to address collusion-related risks.
 
**Recovery actions and Inter-bank coordination**
• The bank is analyzing the incident across Level 1, 2 and 3 scenarios to identify funds not legitimately disbursed.
• Recovery efforts are underway, including from other linked accounts.
• Funds were transferred to multiple other banks, all of other banks which have been cooperative.
• Certain recipient accounts required additional confirmations as part of the recovery process.
• The bank expects further recoveries from the identified accounts.
 
**Controls, checks and preventive framework
**• Transactions above INR 0.5mn are covered under a Positive Pay system, with customer cross-verification.
• Transactions above INR 1mn undergo additional verification layers.
• The bank has introduced a monthly balance monitoring system as part of fraud prevention.
• Going forward, the bank will introduce digitally verified stipulations for transaction authorisation.
• Enhanced measures are being implemented specifically to prevent collusion risks.
• Government accounts continue to receive heightened sensitivity and oversight.
** 
Forensic audit and accountability**
• A forensic audit has been initiated, covering the entire chain of events.
• KPMG has been appointed, with the process expected to take 4–5 weeks.
• The forensic review will verify facts, determine responsibility and assess intent, with a focus on fairness.
• The bank is engaging with employees and counterparties involved and will proceed strictly as per the law.
 
**Financial Impact, Insurance and capital
**• Bank holds an employee dishonesty insurance policy of approximately INR 350mn.
• The bank’s LCR stands at ~115%, well above regulatory requirements.
• Capital adequacy remains comfortable and in excess of minimum norms.
• Core operations remain strong, though the bank may take appropriate provisions.
 
**Business outlook and management confidence
**• Bank maintains a strong reputation in the market, despite recent interest rate reductions.
• Prior to the incident, the bank was expecting a strong quarter, supported by better NIMs and lower credit costs.
• Management reiterated its confidence in the strength of core operations and long-term franchise stability.

**IDFC FIRST BANK CONCALL ON FRAUD OF INR5.9B
** 
**CONCALL KTA
Remarks by management
**• The matter is restricted to the Chandigarh branch, with no involvement of any other branches.
• Employees from this branch were involved and have since been suspended.
• The bank has appointed KPMG to conduct an audit.
• Police complaints have already been filed in connection with the incident.
• Recovery efforts have commenced, and existing control mechanisms remain in place.
• The identified discrepancy stands at INR 4.9bn, with the bank conservatively recognising an additional INR 1bn, taking the total estimated impact to INR 5.9bn. This figure is considered broadly accurate at present.
• The bank remains well-capitalized, with profitability continuing on a positive trajectory.
• This was an isolated branch-level group incident, where debit instructions originated from the client and funds were transferred to external parties, with involvement of branch employees.
• The incident occurred with the connivance of certain employees, and the bank has moved swiftly by initiating a forensic audit.
• Necessary corrective actions will be taken to contain the issue and prevent any recurrence.
• The Board has taken cognisance of the matter and is acting in line with regulatory guidance.
• The bank operates over 1,000 branches and has not encountered a similar fraud in the past.
• The bank expects NIMs of approximately 5.8% for the current quarter.

India's data centers require sophisticated power infrastructure, accounting for nearly 10% of total capital costs, with advanced setups using 2N or N+1 redundancies, modular power/thermal management, and intelligent PDUs for real-time PUE monitoring.

Power Flow Chart
Key Equipment Stages
- Utility to MV: High-voltage step-down via MV transformers and switchgear.
- Redundancy Layer: STS/ATS for seamless failover.
- LV Distribution: Transformers to PDUs feeding IT racks.

Major components include MV transformers, switchgear, protection gear, transfer switches, and PDUs, ensuring reliable power delivery to critical IT loads.

India may revisit the US trade deal from a position of greater leverage.

Price Surge Regains Momentum

Global ETFs Hit Historic Highs  
- World Gold Council: January 2026 saw US$19 bn net inflows – strongest month ever.  
- Combined with 14% gold price rise, global gold ETF AUM reaches record US$669 bn (up 20% m/m).  
- Total holdings approach 4,145 tonnes as investors pile into wealth preservation.  

China Drives ETF Frenzy  
- Chinese gold ETF holdings up 50% in 6 months to 286 tonnes as of Jan 2026.  
- January alone: 38.5 tonnes bought – second-highest ever (after April's 65 tonnes).  
- Retail + institutional rush reflects capital fleeing domestic market stress.  

The Bigger Macro Theme  
- In chaotic, non-linear world: gold = wealth preservation, not just tactical trade.  
- Structural ETF bid + Asian demand provides floor as long as real yields stay suppressed and China exports capital into bullion.  

Key Takeaway: With tariffs dominating headlines, gold's record ETF allocations signal investors prioritizing capital protection over risk assets – a trend likely to persist.

In aerospace, supply bottlenecks create outsized financial returns. Ultimately, scarcity dictates pricing power. Here’s where the squeeze is happening right now:

1) Engines: Pushing the absolute limits of physics and materials creates natural scarcity. Very few companies can build them, which drives high growth and margins for those who do.

2) Aero-structures: The sheer logistical complexity of building and integrating these frameworks limits the supply pool, heavily rewarding the few who can reliably execute.

3) MRO (Maintenance): Aging fleets, high utilization, and widespread engine hiccups have created a perfect storm of demand, opening big doors for localized repair networks.

Source - Anand Rathi

Brokerages On Infosys AI Day

Jefferies
Maintain Buy with TP of Rs 1880

At its AI Day, Infosys highlighted that IT services will remain key to enterprise AI implementation
Infosys has built its AI platform – Infosys Topaz Fabric – is jointly going to market with AI ecosystem partners
Devised a 2-pronged talent strategy not only to capture new service demand but also to augment its existing service lines
Client AI adoption is increasing with new AI services contributing ~5.5% of revenues in Q3FY26
90% of Infosys's top 200 large clients currently using its AI services

BofA
Maintain Buy with TP of Rs 1840

Several positives on positioning
Net opportunity could be bigger but path is still evolving
Enterprise AI isn’t plug and play
Revenue from AI-first offerings stands at 5.5%, not too different from others that have disclosed the metric
Key risk with AI, as per company, is less about opportunity and more about strategy and execution
Anthropic partnerships signify role of a services company in enterprise AI shift

UBS
Maintain Buy with TP of Rs 1870

Opportunities from AI to outweigh compression
AI potential fast expanding, but organisations not ready
US$300-400bn opportunity from AI to outweigh productivity-led compression
IT Service vendors need to build capabilities via talent, platforms / IP, partnerships and acquisitions
Will keep an eye on Infosys' progress on these fronts going ahead

Order Details

• Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited receives project sanction orders from Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA)
• Order includes Pump Modules for Liquid Cooling System
• Applicable for LCA Mk2 and 5th Generation AMCA platforms
• Secured additional orders for Compact Heat Exchangers and Fuel Coolers for AMCA

Strategic Significance

• Expands BHEL’s footprint in high-value defence aerospace systems
• Strengthens participation in India’s indigenous fighter jet programs
• Builds on earlier contribution to LCA Tejas variants
• Positions BHEL in next-generation combat aircraft supply chain

Investor Takeaway

• Entry into advanced thermal management systems
• Enhances defence order visibility
• Supports long-term diversification beyond core power business
• Positive signal for defence revenue scaling over coming years

Trump retains tariff options via established trade laws despite the Supreme Court striking down broad IEEPA use.

Court Ruling Impact
The ruling limits unlimited emergency powers under IEEPA but leaves key statutes intact.

Key Alternatives
Section 301 (Trade Act 1974)  
- Targets unfair foreign practices.  
- Requires USTR investigation and hearings.  
- No tariff size cap; previously used against China.

Section 232 (Trade Expansion Act 1962)  
- National security justification.  
- Needs Commerce Department probe (up to 270 days).  
- Applied to steel/aluminum in first term.

Strategic Implications
Both tools demand formal processes but enable sector-specific duties unaffected by the decision.

Key Takeaway  
Sections 301 and 232 ensure tariff continuity through investigations, preserving trade leverage.
Tariffs can also be imposed under Section 301 and Section 232, but they require a detailed investigation and departmental approval.
They can use SECTION 122 with that 10% tariff can applicable for next 150 days

Emcure Pharma: ₹8,850 Cr TTM Rev, 16.5% 9M Growth

- Domestic: 45%.  
- International complex: 30%.  

Growth & Margins  
- 9MFY26 revenue: +16.5%.  
- Mid-high teens growth last 7 quarters.  
- PAT margins +250 bps (leverage, cost control, productivity).  

Domestic Strength  
- Gynecology, cardio-diabetology: 50% sales.  
- Multiple ₹100 Cr+ brands.  
- New: Poviztra (semaglutide injection) via Manx exclusive license.  
- Expanding: OTC, derm, renal/oncology/CNS.  

International Highlights  
- Canada: Top 10 generics (CAD 236 Mn); 50+ pipeline incl semaglutide.  
- Europe: Injectables growth (Liposomal Amphotericin B, Manx acquisition); 55+ pipeline.  
- RoW: Strong growth; biosimilars/complex injectables (Tenectaplase, Ferric CarboxyMaltose); $2 Bn+ TAM.  

Market Cap  
- ₹28,738.25 Cr.  

Key Takeaway  
Balanced domestic/global mix and pipeline firepower drive Emcure's sustained teens growth with margin tailwinds.

Sigachi Industries Q3/9MFY26 Concall: Expansion Amid Recovery

Q3 FY26 Financials  
- Operating income: ₹117.2 Cr.  
- EBITDA: ₹5.7 Cr (4.6% margin).  
- Net loss: ₹0.02 Cr (-0.01% PAT margin).  

Segment Revenue  
- MCC: ₹61.72 Cr.  
- O&M: ₹13.35 Cr.  
- API: ₹14.13 Cr.  

Operational Updates  
- EHS, discipline, accountability strengthened.  
- Plants at planned capacity; stable supply chain, strong demand.  
- MCC capacity ~18,000 MTPA (~62% exports).  
- MCC utilisation: 70-73% (demand strong, production constrained).  

Expansions  
- 12,000 MTPA MCC at Dahej (Q3 FY27); total 30,000 MTPA.  
- 1,800 MTPA CCS at Dahej SEZ (Q3 FY27).  
- API: R&D/compliance for regulated markets.  
- Hyderabad 6,000 MTPA MCC paused (legal); focus Dahej SEZ.  
- Raw materials shifted to Dahej/Jhagadia.  

Margin Pressures  
- Q3 EBITDA 4.6%: Hyderabad overheads, higher transport, wood pulp custom duty.  
- Gross margins hit by raw material costs.  
- Current segment margins: O&M ~22%, API ~10%, Other ~15%, MCC ~5% (normal >20%).  

Insurance & Balance Sheet  
- Hyderabad claim: Ad hoc ₹20-25 Cr by Mar 31, 2026; total ~₹70 Cr (Fixed ₹48 Cr, Inventory ₹4 Cr, BI ₹25 Cr).  
- No long-term debt; only working capital borrowings.  
- Promoter holding down (pledge invocation); plans to increase.  
- Future capex funding via board decision.  

Outlook & Guidance  
- Q4 FY26 improvement; FY27 recovery; FY28 full normalcy.  
- EBITDA >20% post-normalization; double-digit from FY28.  
- API commercialization: CF in ~12 months (₹250 Cr potential); Metformin CEP approved, others pending.  
- Sustainable growth over 2-3 years; strong FY28 performance.  

Key Takeaway  
Dahej expansions and insurance inflows set Sigachi for FY27 recovery and FY28 double-digit EBITDA rebound.

**Key Takeaway:**  
- Strong orders/EBITDA offset by cautious REDUCE amid high CMP; monitor sector capex execution.

**HDFC Sec on Go Digit: ADD**

**Rating & Target:**  
- Maintains **ADD**; Target **₹350**.  

**Motor Strength:**  
- **Motor TP** flagship with strong GDPI growth.  
- **Commercial lines** gaining share on favorable treaty structure.  

**Challenges:**  
- Motor OD **loss ratio elevated** (aggressive pricing + cat losses).  
- LR correction underway; **12-18 months** timeline.  

**Projections:**  
- NEP/PAT **CAGR 10%****/34****%** over **FY25-28**.  
- Combined ratio improves to **~107%** by FY28.  

**Monitorables:**  
- Growth sustainability + OD profitability.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
- Motor dominance + LR fix supports 34% PAT CAGR to ₹350 target.

**Motilal Oswal Initiates BUY on Lenskart**

**Rating & Target:**  
- CMP: **₹479.90**.  
- Initiate **BUY**; Target **₹600**.  

**Competitive Edge:**  
- Built **strong moats** in difficult-to-scale eyewear category.  

**Growth Projections:**  
- Expect **25% Revenue CAGR** / **53% EBITDA CAGR** over **FY25-28**.

- Near-term FCF impeded by upfront capex on **Hyderabad facility**.  
- FCF to improve to **65-70% of Pre-IND** as EBITDA grows beyond **FY28**.  

**Valuation View:**  
- Premium to other retailers, but **justifiable**.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
- Lenskart’s moats and 25%/53% growth justify premium valuation to ₹600 target.

- Industry supply ~**45 MTPA**; expect **rangebound pricing**.  
- **Consolidation** to prevent significant price falls.

- Expansions skewed to **4Q**.  

**Top Picks:**

**Key Takeaway:**  
- Consolidation cushions pricing amid FY26 supply adds; favor top picks like ULTC and JSW Cement.
